name: The Little Mermaid (Den Lille Havfrue)
description: The iconic bronze statue by Edvard Eriksen sitting on a rock in Copenhagen harbour. Inspired by Hans Christian Andersen's fairy tale. Though small, it is Denmark's most photographed landmark

name: Tivoli Gardens
description: One of the world's oldest amusement parks (1843) and major inspiration for Walt Disney. A magical blend of rides, gardens, restaurants and entertainment. Transforms seasonally — summer, Halloween, Christmas

name: Nyhavn
description: 17th-century waterfront canal lined with brightly coloured townhouses, restaurants and old wooden ships. Once a working port and haunt of Hans Christian Andersen — he lived at nos. 20, 67 and 18

name: Kronborg Castle (Hamlet's Castle)
description: Renaissance castle immortalised as Elsinore in Shakespeare's Hamlet. Guards the narrow strait between Denmark and Sweden. A UNESCO World Heritage Site of extraordinary beauty

name: Roskilde Cathedral
description: Gothic cathedral and UNESCO World Heritage Site that has been the burial site of Danish kings and queens since the 12th century. Contains 40 royal tombs

name: Legoland Billund
description: The original Legoland, opened 1968 in the hometown of LEGO. A beloved family theme park where everything is made from LEGO bricks. Attracts millions of visitors annually

name: Ribe Old Town
description: Denmark's oldest and best-preserved medieval town. Founded around 710 AD. Walking through Ribe is like stepping back in time with its cobblestone streets and Viking museum

name: Den Gamle By (The Old Town Museum)
description: World's first open-air urban history museum. An entire historical town reconstructed with period-accurate shops, homes and workshops spanning Danish history from the 1600s to 1970s

name: Skagen (Grenen)
description: The northernmost tip of Denmark where the North Sea and Baltic Sea visibly meet. A mystical, windswept peninsula beloved by Danish artists. The Skagen Painters made this place world-famous

name: Louisiana Museum of Modern Art
description: One of Scandinavia's most celebrated art museums perched dramatically on the coast of Øresund. World-class collection of modern art in a stunning landscape setting

name: National Gallery of Denmark (SMK)
description: Denmark's largest art museum housing Danish and international art from 700 years of history. Features works by Rembrandt, Rubens, Matisse and the finest collection of Danish Golden Age painting

name: Freetown Christiania
description: A famous self-governing commune established in 1971 by squatters in a former military area. A unique social experiment and Copenhagen's most controversial and colourful neighbourhood
